sprinton.hurst.HurstIntro.1: Greetings. You have found me gathering offerings.$3
sprinton.hurst.HurstIntro.2: I gather offerings to give to the gods every Friday.$3
sprinton.hurst.HurstIntro.3: Hurst seems to be in a good mood
sprinton.hurst.HurstIntro.8: Foraged items, gems, fruit. They value anything, as long as I gather it.$3
sprinton.hurst.HurstIntro.9: I see I have caused offense. I apologize for doing so.
sprinton.hurst.HurstIntro.11: Strange...I cannot hear them. Can you?$0
sprinton.hurst.HurstIntro.12: ...#I can, but I don't understand them.#Yes, but they're speaking a language I don't know.#
sprinton.hurst.HurstIntro.14: Hurst squints, then his eyes widen.
sprinton.hurst.HurstIntro.16: I must go consult the gods. Thank you for speaking with me.$1
sprinton.hurst.Hurst2H.2: What are the spirits you keep talking about?
sprinton.hurst.Hurst2H.5: The spirits are one of the various things I worship. They are creatures of nature and magic.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst2H.6: Yes. Most magic is visible to me. In Mistgrove, the ability to see magic is far more common that it is here.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst2H.7: Somewhat. It is more complicated than merely worshipping the Junimo.$7
sprinton.hurst.Hurst2H.8: I realize this must confuse you. My apologies. I forget the people of this land worship Yoba.$0
sprinton.hurst.Hurst2H.9: In my homeland Mistgrove, we worship anything that is what you would call 'magic'. Spirits are the most common form, but many different types exist.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst2H.10: I give offerings to my shrine, and the gods collect them. I leave some smaller offerings at the households of the other gods and apostles.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst2H.11: I get small blessings in exchange. I always find enough forage to eat. My crops never wither. I rarely fall ill.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst2H.12: Do you still refuse to speak to me?$2
sprinton.hurst.Hurst2H.15: Do you not hear the Junimo?
sprinton.hurst.Hurst2H.16: That's the Junimo from last time!
sprinton.hurst.Hurst2H.17: I cannot hear them.$3#$b#I worry they are angry with me.$2
sprinton.hurst.Hurst2H.19: This Junimo is one I owe a great personal debt.$0#$b#I find their current state alarming.$6#$b#And their refusal to speak with me quite disheartening.$2
sprinton.hurst.Hurst2H.21: You... need... to... stop...$0#$b#The... commandment... is... wrong...$1
sprinton.hurst.Hurst2H.22: Ah, I should let you return to your tasks. It is nice to have another person I am permitted to speak with.$1
sprinton.hurst.Hurst2H.23: ...#What do you mean permitted?#Are you banned from talking to others??#
sprinton.hurst.Hurst2H.24: Yes, my Commandment requires-$3#$b#...$0#$b#Forgive me, I may not speak of such things.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst2H.25: It is nothing. Thank you for speaking with me.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.0: #It's strange seeing you at a Yoba shrine.#What are you doing here?
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.1: I am merely pondering the process of atonement for those who follow Yoba.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.2: How does one get forgiven by Yoba?
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.3: By helping those around you without reward.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.4: By swearing to not repeat the same mistake.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.6: That is quite a noble method.$1
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.8: Asking the gods directly is quite strange to me. $6
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.9: My religion has what we refer to as the 'Commandments of Atonement'. These are commandments given by the spirits, through our prophet.$3#$b#They vary in severity, from Forgiveness to Exile.$3#$b#...My commandment is to be friendless.$2
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.10: I am not permitted to make friends. Speaking beyond polite conversation wtih non-apostles is forbidden. Such is my commandment.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.12: The Prophet can just choose any of the Commandments?
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.14: Why do you speak with me then?
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.15: No. The prophet asks the spirits, who decide on the punishment. The prophet is merely the mouthpiece.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.16: Apostles are what you would call people with magic in their blood, or an affinity toward the gods. The Wizard, for example.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.17: As one who can see the Junimo, you are considered an apostle. But even that status may not be enough.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.18: I tell you this because I fear that our friendship may have violated my Commandment.$2#$b#You likely noticed, but the Pomegranate has been vanishing.$0
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.19: I do not wish to end this friendship. But I fear the repercussions of disobeying the Prophet.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.21: Why do you obey your Prophet to such extremes?
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.22: Have you asked the spirits what they want you to do?
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.23: What do you have to atone for?
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.24: The prophet is the voice of the gods.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.25: To do such a thing would be heresy. The Prophet speaks for the spirits.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.26: ...A grave sin.$2#$b#One I wish to not speak of now.$8
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.27: ...I shall pray to the gods some more. Perhaps I have misunderstood my Commandment.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.28: Either way, I shall leave you be.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.29: I'll be sad to lose such handsome company.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.31: Well, I'm glad to not have to speak with a heretic as much!
sprinton.hurst.Hurst4H.34: I swear I will. Please do the same.$1
FatherJoseph4H.1: I don't quite know what to tell you Hurst. My religion is quite different from yours.$2
FatherJoseph4H.2: I don't want to push you to do anything you don't want to do, but are you sure about this?$2#$b#You seem to be suffering quite a bit.$2
FatherJoseph4H.3: Well if you ever need a listening ear or a warm meal, come on by!$1
WoodsmanHurst.FatherJoseph4H.1: I admit I expected as much. I thank you for trying to help.$3
WoodsmanHurst.FatherJoseph4H.2: Yes. I am certain. I appreciate your concern, but I shall be ok.$1
sprinton.hurst.Hurst5H.0: Ah, my apologies. I did not realize others came this deep into the woods.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst5H.1: I didn't know you came this far either.$0
sprinton.hurst.Hurst5H.2: If you intend to forage here, I shall leave you be.$1
sprinton.hurst.Hurst5H.4: I've seen you in the woods a lot. And you don't ever have that judgemental look in your eyes the others do.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst5H.5: ...Would you like to forage together? It'll be easier with two people. It might even be fun!$1
sprinton.hurst.Hurst5H.7: I...wish I could. But I am not permitted.$2#$b#I apologize.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst5H.9: I shall let you get back to your foraging.$0
sprinton.hurst.Hurst5H.12: Try... to... tell... him... to... stop...
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.4: I am facing a difficult decision.$8
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.5: Some of my truest friends. Friends...whose deaths I caused.$8
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.6: I apologize for you finding me in such a state. I...$8
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.7: I have found myself facing a dilemma regarding my Commandment.$2
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.9: Do we have to stop talking again?
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.10: I fear I will never be able to earn Atonement. I will never be able to let go of this guilt.$2
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.11: No. That was my own hubris causing me to believe I understood the words of the spirits more than Sylva. My fear is I shall never be able to Atone.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.12: To properly understand, I must tell you the tale of my Sin. I warn you, it is quite a grim story.$0
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.13: As one who can hear the spirits, I was destined to be either Prophet or Warden.$0#$b#Prophet was never a role I could truly fulfill, so I was chosen to be Warden.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.14: As Warden of Mistgrove, I had a responsibility of protecting the inhabitants. I would repel evil spirits, or save lost children from them. Rarely, I would even battle demons.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.15: Eventually, rumors of an extremely dangerous demon in the deep forest reached us. With the blessing of Sylva, I took several friends to find and destroy this demon.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.16: We hunted it for years. We started to believe we understood it. We were wrong. When we finally found it, we learned our mistake.$2#$b#We were not the hunter. We were the quarry. I should have realized it as soon as the spirits fled that day, but I believed I could protect us. I was such a fool.$9
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.19: It attacked. I still do not know its true nature. It looked like something from the Void, but far more grotesque.$6
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.20: Yes. Without warning, a creature bearing resemblance to the monsters of the Void attacked us.$8
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.21: We ran. But the demon was fast. It picked us off...one by one.$8#$b#Soon, I was the only survivor. I turned towards the demon. I do not know what I wished to do, I just wanted to make it pay.$5#$b#Before I could do anything however, there was a flash of light, and I awoke in this clearing.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.25: Yes. It saved me by teleporting me here.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.26: No. If I had used any magic, It would have been to destroy the demon at the cost of my life. I was saved by the Pomegranate.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.27: I believe they summoned the Pomegranate, who then saved me.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.28: I owe my life to the Pomegranate. But now I fear I am causing its destruction. I believe my Commandment is harming it.$2
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.29: If such a thing were true, I would be truly dammned. If I disobey, I can never be forgiven of my Sin. If I obey, I destroy the Pomegranate.$8
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.30: You don't have to be forgiven for something that wasn't your fault.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.31: You couldn't have done anything to stop the demon.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.32: Your Commandment is wrong. It's forcing you to atone for something that wasn't your fault.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.33: It was my fault! I lead them there! Besides, I was the *last* one who should have been saved!$5
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.34: I should have been able to! I was Warden for a decade! I should have done *something*!$5
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.35: If it were, it would mean Sylva made a mistake. I do not wish to imagine such a thing.$8
sprinton.hurst.Hurst6H.36: I am sorry, but I would like to be left alone.$8#$b#I have much to think on.$9
sprinton.hurst.Hurst7H.3: ..until he stops ...he'll never let himself... be happy again...$5
sprinton.hurst.Hurst7H.4: He's trying to save you by obeying it! What if he stops and it kills you?
sprinton.hurst.Hurst7H.5: Hurst won't accept that. He thinks it's the only way to atone for his mistakes.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst7H.6: That doesn't matter... he deserves to be happy... besides... his friends... didn't blame him.....$4
sprinton.hurst.Hurst7H.7: It wasn't his fault... he doesn't have to atone... his friends... already forgive him...$4
sprinton.hurst.Hurst7H.8: We're the Ward of Mistgrove... we protect when the Warden can't... we failed...$5
sprinton.hurst.Hurst7H.9: Do you really think Hurst could be happy at the cost of your life?
sprinton.hurst.Hurst7H.10: Why does Hurst have a Commandment like this? It's destroying both of you!
sprinton.hurst.Hurst7H.11: Not at first... but eventually... he'd move on... if he makes friends... they'll help him forgive himself...$4
sprinton.hurst.Hurst7H.12: The Prophet made a mistake.... she chose the wrong Commandment out of anger and pain.... and she can't change a Commandment once given...$7
sprinton.hurst.Hurst7H.13: We're doomed either way... at least if Hurst stops... he can be happy.... eventually.$5
sprinton.hurst.Hurst7H.14: ...#I'll find a way to save you both.#I'm not going to let you both destroy yourselves!
sprinton.hurst.Hurst7H.17: I see why... Hurst cares about you... so much....$6
sprinton.hurst.Hurst7H.18: Maybe... if he stops his Commandment... and he lets go of the guilt himself... we'll be safe...$4
sprinton.hurst.Hurst7H.19: He can't hear us... but he listens to you... convince him... before its too late...$5
sprinton.hurst.Hurst7H.21: We're strong enough to appear one more time... Try to convince him then... or we'll dissappear forever...$4
sprinton.hurst.Hurst7H.22: I need to talk to Hurst.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.0: I apologize for the state I was in during our previous meetings.$0#$b#I have found the solution to my dilemma. I just wish to ask you a simple question now.$0
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.1: Are you certain Ward said the Commandment was destroying them?$7
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.3: If Ward is certain of the cause, then I shall abandon my Commandment.$3#$b#Their life is far more important.$1
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.4: What do you plan on doing now?
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.5: This means you can make friends now, right?
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.6: Does this mean you'll forgive yourself?
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.7: Continue as I have. I will make my offerings. I will obey the spirits. And I will carry my Sin with me forever.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.8: I do not believe so. Even without my Commandment, I am tainted by my Sin. I no longer have a way to absolve myself of it. It is for the best I remain seperate.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.9: No. My Sin will remain with me forever. I have forsaken the way to absolve myself.$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.10: I accept that I will never be fully resolved of my Sin. I am willing to make such a sacrifce to save Ward.$1
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.11: Hurst... that's not... what you need... to do...$5
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.13: You are still... dissappearing.$2#$b#...Is this like the seed shop? I have misunderstood again?$3
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.14: He still... can't hear us... please... help us....$1
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.15: What do they mean? What am I supposed to do?$6
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.17: What did they say? Did I make another mistake? Did I fail once again? Have I damned us both?$6
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.18: You have to let go of the guilt Hurst.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.19: You need to stop blaming yourself.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.20: You have to be willing to move on.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.21: I am trying! That is the purpose of my Commandment! It allows me to earn forgiveness!$3#$b#...At least, so I thought.$8
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.22: ...How?$2#$b#That was what my Commandment was supposed to do!$5#b#...That is what my Commandment was... supposed to do.$8
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.23: How am I to move on?$8#$b#How am I to forget the sounds of my friends? Their faces in the end? The terror of-$5#$b#...$8#b#...The Commandment was supposed to help.$8
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.24: Hurst lowers his voice to barely a whisper.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.25: A Commandment is the only way I can atone. That is the only way that...$8#$b#...They would forgive me.$9
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.27: They don't... blame you... they already... forgave you... Hurst...$5
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.28: How do you know such a thing? You were too late to save them. You could not have seen how it happened.$8#$b#You did not see the blame in their eyes. The fear. The pain. The betrayal.$9
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.29: Hurst... listen... to.. them...$5#$b#You... can't hear... us...$5#$b#But maybe... you can hear... them.$4
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.32: Aera... Eon... Merri...$5#$b#They've been... following him... for years...$4#$b#They just... forgot who... they were... until now...$5
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.33: But... The Commandments! That is how we are absolved! That is how I can earn your forgiveness!$9
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.34: I understand, but...$8#$b#But...$9#$b#I cannot do what you wish.$9#$b#I cannot accept it.$9
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.35: Hurst... we don't... have anymore... time...$5#$b#We're sorry... we couldn't... save them...$5
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.36: I do not deserve this.$9#$b#I do not deserve their forgiveness.$9#$b#I do not deserve your friendship.$9#$b#I do not deserve to be happy.$9
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.37: Why do you all believe I deserve this? I failed them.$8#$b#I failed Sylva. I failed Mistgrove.#$9#$b#I promised I would get them home safe. How can I accept that?$8#$b#How can I forgive myself for that?$9
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.38: Your friends forgave you Hurst. Ward forgave you. If you really want to 'earn' that, live how they want you to.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.39: You know you couldn't have saved them. Nothing you did would have stopped the demon. You know Sylva made a mistake when she gave you that Commandment. The spirits have told you as much.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.40: You deserve it because everyone deserves a chance to happy. Everyone deserves to be loved.
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.41: I wish to. I truly want to earn their forgiveness. But...$9
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.42: The demon was far stronger than we thought, was it not?$1#$b#I do not know if I could have even defeated it head on.$9
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.43: Everyone deserves a chance at love.#$8#$b#Everyone deserves to be happy.$1
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.45: Not today, at least.$2#$b#But I swear to you, to Ward, to everyone that I will.$1#$b#One day, I will forgive myself. I will learn to let go of this guilt. I promise.$1
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.47: I can hear you, my friend.$1
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.48: You can hear us! You can hear us!$2#$b#...But we failed. Why do you call us friend?$1
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.49: If I am to forgive myself for my failure, you must forgive yourself as well.$0#$b#Perhaps we can walk the path to forgiveness together.$1
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.50: You truly are the most marvelous person I have ever met. I owe you far more than I could ever repay.$1#$b#I swear to repay my debt one day. Unfortunately, today is a day for conversing with old friends. I have many things to speak with Ward and the Shopkeepers about.$1
sprinton.hurst.Hurst8H.P1.51: If we can ever help you, just ask!$2
HurstJojaWarehouseNoWard.0: Amazing, isn't it? Lewis finally put some good use to that old waste of a building.$0
HurstJojaWarehouseNoWard.1.true: You are a true heretic Morris.$5
HurstJojaWarehouseNoWard.2: I admit, you gave me quite a shock. You bursting into the JojaMart was a surprise!$0#$b#...I am displeased at how your yelling disturbed my customers however.$3
HurstJojaWarehouseNoWard.3: At least you didn't try to stop the renovation. I did ask them to keep an eye out, but you didn't show.$0#$b#Pity. I would've enjoyed seeing you charged for trespassing.$1
HurstJojaWarehouseNoWard.4: Come on! Aren't you gonna yell at me about your 'gods' again? Tell me they're going to condemn me?
HurstJojaWarehouseNoWard.5: ...I wish the gods could explain how such a creature like you found your way to such a beautiful place like this valley.$2#$b#I have nothing left to say to a creature such as you.$2
HurstJojaWarehouseNoWard.6: I hope you are safe, my gods. I am sorry for being unable to defend you from such a creature.
HurstJojaWarehouseNoWard.7: Joja will be good for the town! Your 'gods' do nothing! Your 'offerings' are a waste!$3

HurstJojaWarehouseWard.8: I will miss them as well.$2

WoodsmanHurst.AcceptGift_Pomegranate.fal: This fruit reminds me of...$8#$e#My apologies. I love this gift.$4
WoodsmanHurst.AcceptGift_Pomegranate.tru: Ah, this fruit reminds me of Ward.$0#$e#They bear a strong resemblance to Pomegranates.$1
WoodsmanHurst.AcceptGift_FairyDust: What a gift! The dust of a fairy!$6#$e#I have rarely seen so much of it before!$1
WoodsmanHurst.AcceptGift_SweetGemBerry: I love this fruit.$0#$e#Nothing else is quite as sweet as it.$7

WoodsmanHurst.AcceptGift_SmolderingRose: I find the embers of the rose to be beautiful.$0#$e#I would often use these for light while fulfilling my role as the Warden.$1

WoodsmanHurst.AcceptGift_TeardropSpirits: This is my favorite drink.$4#$e#After drinking it, the water spirits lessen their hatred towards me.$1#$e#I wish I knew what I did to offend them so.$2

WoodsmanHurst.AcceptGift.MountainMistblo: This plant has a similar essence to those of my home Mistgrove.$0#$e#I thank you for bringing me such a gift.$1
WoodsmanHurst.DumpsterDiveComment: I will not judge your choices.$0#$b#...Even if they seem strange.$7
WoodsmanHurst.PacifistGiftResponse: My apologies, but I cannot accept this.$0#$e#It brings bad memories.$2
WoodsmanHurst.AcceptGift_StardropTea: What a gift!$1#$e#I shall request the spirits of growth to bless your harvest.$0

WoodsmanHurst.RejectMermaidPendant: I must decline. I do not follow such practices.$0

WoodsmanHurst.EggFestival.1: Greetings. Are you going to particpate in the hunt?$0#$b#If so, I wish you luck. I have heard tales of how proficient a hunter Abigail is.$1

JunimoWard.Luau: Everyone's making one big present! That's so cool!$2

WoodsmanHurst.IceFestival: I worry Ward will try to sneak a fish home again.$0#$b#Last time they found a fish, they had Rasmodius hide it, even from my sight.$0#$b#The smell stuck in the clearing for nearly a week afterwards.$2

JunimoWard.WinterStar2: Hurst gave us a Starfruit as a present today. We love Starfruit!$2

WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.1: I must gather my offerings.$0#$e#I have yet to fail at giving a proper offering, and do not wish to start now.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.2: The spirits have been restless recently.$0#$e#It has been some time without someone new.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.3: Do you deal with those at blue demon den?$7#$e#I warn you to be cautious of them.$0
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.4: I usually keep my emotions under control, but the demon Joja always makes my temper flare.$0#$e#I do not wish violence on them, but small annoyances would be a joyous event.$1#$b#A pipe bursting, perhaps.$3
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.5: I saw a spirit of growth swirling around %farm farm.$0#$e#They can be quite fickle.$3
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.6: The spirits swirl about you nearly constantly.$0#$e#It seems they find you fascinating.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.7: I fear for what would happen if the demon known as Joja gets a stronger foothold in the town.$3#$e#It seems to be opposed to the gods.$5
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.8: The gods speak of your grandfather fondly.$0#$e#I wonder kind of man he was to leave such an impression.$3
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.9: Rasmodius is quite powerful.$0#$e#The only other Apostle I have met with his control over magic is the Prophet Sylva.
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.10: I wonder what the gods do with the offerings.$0#$e#There is never anything left behind.$3
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.11: I try to keep my clearing from becoming overgrown.$0#$e#It can be difficult at times.$7
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.12: The artist who lives by the ranch is extremely talented.$0#$e#Everytime I see her, spirits of art and creativity are all around her.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.13: I spend Sundays wandering the valley.$0#$e#I enjoy seeing the beauty of the land.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.14: Do you enjoy seeing nature?$0#$e#Very few things in this world are quite as beautiful.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.15: Have you grown any Starfruit?$0#$e#The gods of sun and warmth love them so.$3
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.16: A new god has revealed itself to me.$0#$e#Even now, I do not know how many types of gods exist.$3
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.17: Sometimes the gods confuse me.$2#$e#I forget they do not fully understand mortals.$3
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.18: Do you know any of the spirits?$3#$e#They have been more active since you arrived.$0
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.19: I think I saw a spirit of rain this morning.$3#$e#I suggest keeping an eye on the sky.$0#$b#They enjoy playing pranks.$6
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.20: The cart merchant sometimes wanders into my clearing.$0#$e#They have an fascinating creature as a pet.$0
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.21: Foraging the deep woods reminds me of my time as Warden.$0#$e#The forest here is not nearly as spiritually active however.$3
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.22: Have you seen the goats that wander nearby?$0#$e#They are quite fascinating creatures.$3
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.23: The Spirit of Mystery has been leaving boxes in my clearing.$5#$e#I truly hate that creature.$5
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.24: I believe the creature Morris has been trying to expand his demons influence further through the town.$5#$e#I have asked the gods to try to stop him.$3
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.25: The children spoke of exploring the deep woods yesterday.$0#$e#Should I leave a note to warn their parents of such dangerous plans?$7
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.26: I have decided to learn more about this Yoba.$0#$e#I am curious what kind of spirit takes on such a strange way of worship.$0
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.27: That creature Morris was prowling around my clearing yesterday.$5#$e#I do not know much about the demon Joja, but I know it and gods are opposed.$7
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.28: When I returned to my clearing the other day, there was a doll sitting there.$0#$e#Does it belong to one of the children?$7
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.29: The carpenter of the mountain has made many fascinating projects.$0#$e#She is quite talented.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.30: Have you met the Spirit of Mystery?$0#$e#He is a truly wicked creature.$5
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.31: While I was wandering the woods, I encountered a bear.$6#$e#It asked if I had any maple syrup?$7#$b#...was it one of the gods in disguise?.$3
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.32: The gods told me you wander the mines.$0#$e#I warn you to be careful, the spirits there can be quite dangerous to humans.$2
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.33: The mountains nearby are quite beautiful, are they not?$0#$e#I think the gods enjoy this land due to the abundance of nature.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.34: The gods do not know the name Yoba.$0#$e#I wonder what kind of creature can escape their notice?$3
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.35: The ranch is quite an interesting place.$0#$e#There is always a massive collection of spirits swirling about.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.36: I admire the florists talent with flower displays.$0#$e#There is always great beauty in her work.
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.37: On occasion, I have taken in injured animals to the veterinarian.$0#$e#His daughter enjoys sharing her knowledge of birds with me whenever I do so.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.38: I find the slime breeder quite interesting.$0#$e#She seems to have no fear for the dangerous creatures!$6
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.39: The ocean somewhat terrifies me.$0#$b#As a child, I was nearly drowed by a violent ocean spirit.$6#$e#I still do not know what I had done to offend her so.$2
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.40: Rasmodius has asked me to teach him how to speak with the spirits.$0#$e#He fails to understand it is not a talent I can teach.$3
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.41: %Hurst seems to be speaking to the air.$0#$e#I must ask you to not do such a thing. The people here will fail to understand it is a prank.$3
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.42: Are you close with the townsfolk?$0#$e#They are all good people, are they not? I sometimes wish I could be permitted to speak to them so.$2
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.43: %Hurst is staring into the distance.#$e#I sometimes worry my atonement will never end.$2
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.44: I wish I could show you the spirits I see in this valley.$0#$e#They are quite a sight to behold, especially whenever you are nearby.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.45: I have heard stories that the woman that works at the saloon has a friend in the desert.$0#$e#The concept of a desert is one I find quite strange. How can a place be so dry all the time?$6
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.46: In my home of Mistgrove, nearly everyone has the ability to see the spirits.$0#$e#It was quite strange to move here, where it seems no one has the same sight.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.47: The cemetery being in the center of town reminds me of the one back home.$0#$e#We have it in a similar place, due to it being the only place free of constant mist.$3
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.48: Mistgrove is quite a different place from here.$0#$e#We do not have nearly as advanced technology as you.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.49: I miss the lanterns of home at times.$0#$e#Instead of metal and glass, our laterns are woven from vines, with fireflies that live within.$0
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.50: Your shrine to Yoba being tucked away is strange to me.$0#$e#We in Mistgrove have the shrines to the gods in the center of town, where any can access them.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.51: The buildings here are quite different from my homeland.$0#$e#We live mostly in the trees themselves, or out in the fields.$0
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.52: My home Mistgrove is constantly covered in a deep mist.$0#$e#It makes a place like this quite strange to me at times.$0
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.53: When I lived in Mistgrove, my task was to wander the forest.$0#$e#There were spirits there who could be quite dangerous to the young. Someone must keep an eye out for them.$3
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.54: Upon my arrival, I mistook the artist as one from Mistgrove.$0#$e#Purple eyes are a shared trait by those from that land.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.55: The gods of this land are quite different from what I am used to.$0#$e#The gods here have far less variety than those I am accustomed to.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.56: Mistgrove is quite beautiful at night.$0#$e#The mist and the fireflies make quite a sight to behold.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.57: If I were to get my hands on some of my homelands crops, would you be willing to grow them?$0#$e#I would love to be able to taste a Teardrop Berry again.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.58: I have asked if the gods would be willing to bless me with some Terra Gourd seeds.$0#$e#The plant itself is bland, but it can make a delicous stew.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.59: Have you ever seen a picture of a Zephyr Lily?$0#$e#They are quite beautiful flowers. I hope you are able to see one someday.$1
WoodsmanHurst.LowHearts.60: I wish I could see a Smoldering Rose again.$0#$e#I find very few flowers to be nearly as beautiful.$1

WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.2: Rasmodius is one of the few I have met who knows of Mistgrove.$0#$e#Even then, he knows very little about my homeland.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.3: I find the woman of the ranch fascinating.$0#$e#She is beloved by the animals.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.4: Some of the gods have told me their true names.$0#$e#It is a great honor to be given such knowledge.$3
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.5: As Warden, I was very comfortable with seeing large amounts of spirits.$0#$e#The sheer amount of spirits about the Community Center still gives me pause, however.$3
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.6: The gods told me about a strange cave near the train.$0#$e#I was unable to locate it unfortunately.$2
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.7: I admire your talents at farming.$0#$e#I can barely make a simple crop grow without the gods assistance, while you manage the entirety of %farm.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.8: The children wandered into my clearing yesterday.$0#$e#They found my shrine to be intriguing.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.9: I visited the doctor Harvey the other day.$0#$e#I usually care for my injuries myself, but this one was quite extreme.$0
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.10: I wonder if Aideen would be willing to explain to me how she cares for her plants.$0#$e#Her flowers are quite beautiful.
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.11: I find the ocean to be beautiful.$0#$e#I cannot swim, but I enjoy looking out at how far it extends.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.12: Have you seen the creature in the water?$0#$e#The gods say it is a friend of theirs.$3
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.13: On my way through the forest the other day, some slimes attacked me.$6#$e#The gods protected me, however.$4
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.14: The gods sometimes 'attach' themselves to a person or family.$4#$e#The Prophet Sylva experienced such an event in her youth.$3
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.15: I saw the artist working on her statue a few days ago.$0#$e#She is very talented at woodworking.$0$e#I wonder if she could make a charm for the gods?$3
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.16: @, I must ask you, do you understand children?$0#$e#The children asked me why I play pretend all the time.$7#$b#They failed to understand my religion, even after I tried to explain.$2
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.17: Mistgrove is a beautiful place.$0#$e#One day, I hope you will be able to see it.$6
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.18: Do you enjoy the festival on the beach?$0#$e#It reminds me of a similar festival we would hold in Mistgrove.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.19: Sight-Mother was a farmer like you. She was quite talented at it.$0#$e#I think you would have enjoyed learning how to grow the crops of Mistgrove from her.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.20: The woman Jessie is interesting.$0#$e#I find her way of taking those in need under her care quite admirable.
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.21: The creature Morris has stopped coming near my clearing.$1#$e#I believe the gods have managed to scare it off.$3
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.22: If you ever have a chance to try it, Sylph Tea is a delicious drink.$0#$e#The flavor is bitter, but it makes you feel light as air.
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.23: There have been spirits swirling about one of the houses in town.$0#$e#It is quite strange. Those specific spirits are typically found near slimes.$3
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.24: There was a strange creature wandering about town last night.$0#$e#I could not see it, but there was some magic around it.$7#$b#It fled into the sewers.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.25: My home has a drink called Teardrop Spirits.$0#$e#I do not enjoy it very much, but the fisherman of my town said it helped their skills improve.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.26: That woman Caroline showed me how to use a plant I had been gathering a few days ago.$1#$e#I must admit, the drink it made was quite enjoyable.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.27: Have there been any strange occurences on %farm farm?$0#$e#If the gods start to play tricks on you, I shall ask them to stop.$3
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.28: The Spirit of Mystery visited me last night.$6#$e#I fail to understand understand how such a creature can exist.$5
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.29: @!$0#$e#You must have had a good day!$0#$b#A spirit of joy is swirling around you!$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.30: The gods sometimes inhabit my shrines statues.$0#$e#They have been asking for 'a fruit of gems'.$0#$b#I am not sure what that means.$7
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.31: The gods have sometimes moved the statues of my shrine around.$0#$e#They enjoy playing pranks on me at times.$0
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.32: Be careful in the woods.$1#$e#The forest itself can be dangerous, and the spirits are especially powerful here.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.33: I have asked Leah for some assistance in maintaining my shrine.$0#$e#It is hard to protect the colors of the statues from the elements.$0
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.34: Keeping my shrine clean can be a challenge sometimes.$0#$e#I often find leaves hidden beneath the statues of the gods.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.35: Do you grow Pumpkins?$0#$e#I have a personal love for them.
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.36: I spoke to Jacob.$0#$e#He says the animals I have brought in the past have made a good recovery.$0#$e#I am glad.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.37: Sometimes I wander the deep forest.$0#$e#It can be dangerous, but there is a strange beauty in the danger of nature, is there not?$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.38: The spirits tell me your grandfather occasionaly saw them.$0#$b#Such a person is quite special. I believe you inherited your gift of seeing Junimo from him.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.39: I sometimes consider asking for supplies to clean the bathhouse.$0#$e#I use it somewhat often. I believe my cleaning it would be proper payment.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.40: The Spirit of Mystery and I got into a slight...disagreement the other day.$7#$e#I do not think any creature confuses me such as he.$5
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.41: Have you met the god of the mountain?$0#$e#They speak a strange language even I have trouble understanding at times.$2
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.42: Have you ever tried to swim in the nearby ocean?$0#$e#I can only assume it is quite a wonderful experience.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.43: There is a strange power in the nearby desert.$0#$e#I can feel something craving the power of the sun.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.44: I have tried to speak to the gods about Yoba.$0#$b#I do not think they understand my confusion. Maybe Yoba is another one of them?$3
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.45: I asked the spirits to bless your crops.$1#$e#You have been kind to me, it is the least I can do as thanks.$0
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.46: I sometimes wonder if the gods have gods of their own.$1#$e#What kind of entity would they worship?$7
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.47: The idols of the gods in my clearing sometimes play small tricks on me.$0#$b#They dance about behind me, but when I turn around, they return to their dormant selves.$2
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.48: Several of the gods have talked about your adventures.$0#$e#They enjoy watching your daily activities.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.49: As one who speaks with the spirits, I do not say this lightly.$0#$e#I have never met someone who the spirits love as much as you.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.50: Have you been to the distant island?$0#$e#The gods talk about it occasionally, but I have never been myself.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.51: The gods talk about a strange rainbow gem.$0#$e#They say if you offer it to the god of the desert, they will bless you with an offering of their very own.$7
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.52: I have sometimes found strange spirits near the cave on the beach.$0#$e#I wonder if there is something that attracts them.$7
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.53: There exist demons as well as gods.$2#$e#I am sure you have met a few during your exploration of the mines.$0#$b#We must thank Rasmodius for keeping this valley safe from them.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.54: The gods have allowed me to harness their powers once or twice.$0#$e#The Spirit of Mystery can somehow do it without their permission.$6#$b#That is part of why I find him so distasteful.$5
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.55: How has your %farm farm been recently?$0#$e#I have done my best to convice the gods of the storm to leave your farm be.$0#$b#They enjoy striking crops with lightning.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.56: That couple Evelyn and George have been here for quite a long time.$0#$e#I wonder what they have seen in their lives.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.57: I sometimes wonder what would have happened if I had been born without my ability to hear the spirits.$0#$b#Perhaps I would never have come to this place, and never have met you.$0
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.58: Marlon told me he has seen the gods once or twice.$0#$e#I wonder what caused him to be able to see them.$3
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.59: I saw the scientist in the woods a few days ago.$0#$b#He said he was 'gathering samples of the odd organic mutations' that grow around my clearing.$7
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.60: The writer is an entertaining person, is he not?$1#$b#I spoke with him while at the beach a few days ago.$0#$b#He tells such fascinating stories.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.61: The young man Alex is quite energetic.$0#$b#Nearly every time I see him, he is doing something athletic.$0#$b#I can respect such a dedication to ones lifetsyle.$3
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.62: Sebastian is quite closed off.$0#$b#I realize the irony in me saying such a thing, but I hope he is well. The spirits around him are odd.$3
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.63: The children of Mistgrove would play a dangerous game. They would run deep into the woods. The one who went the deepest won.$0#$b#I feel guilty, but I am the reason they stopped. I did not force them to, but one of the children ran directly into me. I believe I was quite intimidating in her eyes.$2
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.64: Maru is quite an intelligent person.$1#$b#Technology and I have always been at odds, but I find her ability to create such marvels quite respectable. I wonder what the gods think of her creations?$3
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.65: Penny seems to be quite good with children.$0#$b#I wonder if she would do well with the gods?$7#$b#They can be quite childlike in their activities.$3
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.66: The man George is quite harsh, but I believe he is kind.$0#$b#He always seems to have at least a couple of spirits around him.$3
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.67: The gods speak of a strange spirit within the sewer.$3#$b#I have noticed traces of the void emerging from the grate. I do not think I shall try to explore it.$2
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.68: Tales of the old Watchers are passed down from master to student.$0#$e#One of the first Watchers was said to have carried a massive blade. It was said it was too big to even be called a blade.
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.69: The shopkeeper once tried to convice me his shop was better than the demon den.$0#$e#I think he forgot I do not use money.$0#$b#He was right about his shop being superior, however.$0#$b#...even if that is not much of an accomplishment.$2
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.70: I have spoken with the fisherman once or twice.$0#$b#Unfortunately, my dislike of fish made our conversations quite awkward.$2
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.71: While I miss my home, I must admit living here is quite peaceful.$0#$e#The spirits have far more sway in Mistgrove, and sometimes that leads to...incidents.$2
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.72: Sylva is quite beloved by my homeland.$0#$e#The spirits about her are quite rare.$0
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.73: Those who are beloved by the spirits draw more of them.$0#$e#You have more about you than anyone else I have ever met.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.74: Farmers are quite rare in Mistgrove.$0#$e#Most of our food comes from forage.$1
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.75: I think you would enjoy Mistgrove.$0#$e#The people there are kind hearted, much like you.$5
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.76: The gods have spoken to me about the offerings.$0#$e#They enjoy them, but seem to think I should not push myself to exhaustion for them.$3#$b#How benevolent!$3
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.77: Rasmodius has a few strange spirits near his tower.$0$e#I have tried to speak with them, but they would not answer me.$3
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.78: When I was still Warden, I would sometimes cross over into the worlds of the spirits.$0#$e#They are quite beautiful, but extremely dangerous.$3
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.79: The gods showed me their world once.$0#$e#It is quite a beautiful, if confusing, place.$3
WoodsmanHurst.HighHearts.80: Wandering the mist is something I miss about my home.$0#$e#Seeing deep purples of the mist is a sight to behold.$1
WoodsmanHurst.Rain: The rain is quite beautiful.$0#$e#It sometimes reminds me of Mistgrove.$1
WoodsmanHurst.BundleOverride1: The gods asked me to gather this for you.
WoodsmanHurst.BundleOverride2: $0#$b#I hope it is useful for you.
WoodsmanHurst.Dating.1: %Hurst stares at you without saying anything.$0#$e#My apologies. I wished to enjoy...you.$4
WoodsmanHurst.Cambria.1: I nearly drowned within the witches river trying to obtain a void salmon for Cambria.$7#$e#I try to avoid such places, but I wished to repay her kindness towards me.$4
WoodsmanHurst.Cambria.2: Sometimes I fear for Cambria.$0#$e#Such love for void salmon...$2#$e#It cannot be safe.$3
WoodsmanHurst.Cambria.3: The bird that accompanies Cambria is quite odd.$0#$e#I can see the aura of magic around it, but cannot determine what causes such an extreme aura.$7
WoodsmanHurst.Cambria.4: The cove is quite a beautiful place.$0#$e#Cambria has invited me to her home on occasion. Typically whenever I have been unable to obtain enough food for myself.$1
WoodsmanHurst.Cambria.5: There is an powerful magic surrounding Cambria. I believe it strengthens her.$0#$e#I do not think she is the source of it however. I wonder what is?$7
WoodsmanHurst.Cambria.6: %Hurst beckons you closer, lowering his voice to be barely audible and glances around furtively.$0#$e#Farmer, if I am to disappear, find Orchid. She will most likely be the cause.$2
WoodsmanHurst.CamieIntro: Cambria is quite kind.$0#$b#I worry for her however. Her bird is quite...odd.$0
WoodsmanHurst.CamieOrchidKnife: I once told Cambria that her companion seemed more than merely a bird.$0#$b#When I arrived at home, there was a single knife laying on my shrine.$6#$b#The message was clear.$0
WoodsmanHurst.EnD.1: The apostle Eli has bombarded me with questions about Mistgrove.$1#$e#I hope he is not too disappointed when he learns I am not permitted to take him there.$2
WoodsmanHurst.EnD.2: I often see the spirits following Eli around.$0#$e#I wonder what he has done to draw their attention?$7
WoodsmanHurst.EnD.3: The gods have told me that Eli has a strange connection to the void.$0#$e#Normally such a thing would make me wary, but Eli is a kind soul.$1
WoodsmanHurst.EnD.4: Yet another conversation with Eli ended with me explaining I cannot merely teach him how to speak with spirits.$0#$e#It is a rare gift, bestowed by the gods.$3
WoodsmanHurst.EnD.5: Those of Mistgrove would have liked the elder Moray.$0#$e#He reminds me of the previous Warden, always energetic and curious to explore.$1
WoodsmanHurst.EnD.6: I find Dylan to be quite fascinating.$0#$e#If I were to attempt exploring the oceans depths as he has, the gods of water would surely keep me there forever.$3
WoodsmanHurst.EnD.7: Dylan was watching the pools on the beach a few days ago.$0#$e#I do not know much about what lives inside of them, but it looks quite fascinating.$1
WoodsmanHurst.EnD.8: The younger Moray would have been a good Warden, if he were born into such a life.$0#$e#He has the calm demeanor required for the most dangerous aspects of such a role.$3
WoodsmanHurst.EnD.9: The fish of Mistgrove are unlike any Dylan has ever encountered.$7#$e#I spoke of them in conversation with him, and ended up being interviewed for nearly an hour.$1
WoodsmanHurst.EnD.10: Sometimes I wish Mistgrove would permit me to send visitors.$0#$e#There exist some fish I think Dylan enjoy to study.$1
WoodsmanHurst.DylanIntro: You spoke with Dylan?$0#$b#He seems to know as much about fish as I do spirits.$1
WoodsmanHurst.EliIntro: You have met the Apostle Eli?$0#$b#He is quite an interesting man. He know far more about the gods than I thought one of this land would.$3
WoodsmanHurst.EliJunimo2: One of the gods seems to have taken an interest in Eli.$3#$b#I wonder what they want from him?$7
WoodsmanHurst.ThistleIntro.false: Have you encountered the crow that follows Dylan about?$0#$b#I wonder if it is some kind of familiar.$7
WoodsmanHurst.ThistleIntro.true: Have you encountered the crow that follows Dylan about?$7#$b#I fear it may be the same kind of creature as Orchid.$6#$b#I hope it is not. I do not need another creature to fear.$2
WoodsmanHurst.ValIntro: You were saved by Val? That is good.$0#$b#I often hear of them from the spirits of the deep mines.$3
WoodsmanHurst.ValEmertiusIntro: Val has somehow tamed a monster.$0#$b#I believe they will ensure Emeritus not pose a threat to those in the valley.$1
WoodsmanHurst.ValMinibossBattle: You and Val encountered a demon on the beach?$0#$b#I hope you keep yourselves safe. Such creatures existing in this town concern me greatly.$2
WoodsmanHurst.TCG_TwinsLeave: Joseph has been quite sad without his grandchildren.$2#$b#I hope he can see them again soon.$1
WoodsmanHurst.NoraIntro: I have often seen the woman Nora in the woods.$0#$b#She seems to have trouble hearing. I mistakenly scared her once while walking past.$2
AliyahTherapyShoo: Farmer, I am sorry, but these sessions are confidential. I can catch up with you later, if you like.$15
WoodsmanHurst.Therapy.1: I worry about never being able to forgive myself. What if I fail?$8
WoodsmanHurst.Therapy.2: I still remember the looks on their faces. I know they have told me they do not blame me. But how can I forget that fear?$8
WoodsmanHurst.Therapy.3: Sylva is not perfect. I know this. But she is still my Prophet. It is hard to find myself disagreeing with her.$2
WoodsmanHurst.Therapy.4: The spirits tell me that this seems to be helping me. I admit it has been. I appreciate your help very much.$4
WoodsmanHurst.Therapy.5: Sylva was my closest friend when we were children. I was quite... bad at making friends as a child. She pushed me to be better at it.$1
WoodsmanHurst.Therapy.6: When I first arrived here, I was too afraid to be alone in the dark. I would... remember.$2
WoodsmanHurst.Therapy.7: Aera, Merri, and Eon are some of the kindest people I have ever met. I...$2
WoodsmanHurst.Therapy.8: My master sacrificed herself to protect me. It is something of a tradition for my role. Each mentor dying to save their apprentice.$9
WoodsmanHurst.Therapy.9: Sometimes I wish I could return to the town in Mistgrove itself. At other times I am glad I am exiled. Is that not strange?$6
WoodsmanHurst.Therapy.10: Ward is glad I have been attending. I have been telling them of our sessions. They seem to believe it helps them as well.$1
WoodsmanHurst.Aliyah.1: I owe nearly as great a debt to Aliyah as I do you. She has helped me improve greatly.$0#$e#I admire her ability to help me understand how I can improve.$1
WoodsmanHurst.Aliyah.2: Often I have found the wolves gathering near Aliyah. She seems to have a natural affinity with them.$1#$e#Such a talent is wonderful.$1
WoodsmanHurst.Aliyah.3: The suprise I felt at first meeting Aliyah was quite extreme.$0#$e#I have never met one as tall as she.$6
WoodsmanHurst.Aliyah.4: I have been trying to ensare a rabbit. Aliyah mentioned her fondness of them.$0#$e#I have not been successful in my attempts. They are quite fast creatures.$1
WoodsmanHurst.Aliyah.5: There is a powerful spirit about Aliyah. I sometimes find myself lost staring at it when I speak with her.$0#$e#I worry she might think our conversations bore me due to this.$2
WoodsmanHurst.Dahlia.1: Dahlia seemed to think I misunderstood the idols of the gods.$7#$e#She referred to them as 'plushies'. I worry I have confused her in some way.$2
WoodsmanHurst.Dahlia.2: It appears Dahlia has undertaken a quest to befriend every resident of the Scarp.$0#$e#Her friendliness is something I find wonderful.$1
WoodsmanHurst.Dahlia.3: The love Dahlia has for snow is quite fascinating to me. I had never encountered snow before arriving in the valley.$1#$e#It appears to me she feels as comfortable in the snow as I do in the woods.$1
WoodsmanHurst.Dahlia.4: My old master told me tales of Dahlia's people, but I never expected to meet one of them.$0#$e#I hope I can remember to show the proper respect to her when needed.$3
WoodsmanHurst.Dahlia.5: Sometimes I wonder if I should bring offerings to Dahlia. She is...strange to me.$3#$e#Is she a god? Or something else entirely? I do not know.$7
WoodsmanHurst.LucikielArrives: A new god appears!$6#$b#Lucikiel was it? I must gather the proper offerings!$3
WoodsmanHurst.MeetTheCats: The cats speak.$0#$b#Are they another type of god?$7#$b#I shall prepare offerings just in case.$3
JunimoWard.DailyPool.1: We first saw Hurst when he was a kid.$0#$e#The old Warden found him being attacked by a demon!$3#$b#He doesn't talk about that story a lot.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.2: Hurst really cares about you!$0#$e#Everytime we visit him, he's thinking about you!$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.3: We hid a fish in Hurst's tent.$2#e#He doesn't like fish.$0#$b#We can't wait to see his face when he finds it!$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.4: Hurst has started to leave us presents too!$0#$e#We really love when he leaves us Starfruit!$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.5: Have you ever met Hurst's spirits? They're green!$0#$e#They're really friendly. They like you a lot!$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.6: We sometimes sneak into Hurst's sleeping bag at night.$0#$e#It's really warm in there!$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.7: Have you ever tried to talk to the Shopkeepers?$0#$e#They don't always remember themselves. It makes us sad when they forget us.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.8: The water spirits are really friendly!$0#$e#They like nearly everyone! When people swim they get happy!$0#$b#They don't like Hurst though.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.9: Sometimes we try to talk to the little plushies Hurst has.$0#$e#We can feel some strange magic coming off of them.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.10: We visit Hurst on the weekends.$0#$b#We get lonely without him.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.11: We wish you could have met Sylva before all this happened.$0#$b#You would've been good friends.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.12: Sylva noticed us when we were walking outside of Mistgrove yesterday.$0#$e#She didn't try to talk to us though. We think she feels guilty.$0
JunimoWard.DailyPool.13: Did you know Sylva and Hurst were best friends as kids?$2#$3#They would wander the deep woods together!$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.14: Not too long ago Sylva tried to talk to us.$0#$b#She asked about the valley.$0#$b#She was surprised at how many magical people live there.$0
JunimoWard.DailyPool.15: We had to convince Sylva to let you visit here.$0#$b#Mistgrove itself is still banned, but the Outskirts are allowed.$0
JunimoWard.DailyPool.16: We worry about Sylva. If something happens to us, she has to be the Prophet and the Warden.$0#$e#Hopefully we'll stay safe.$3
JunimoWard.DailyPool.17: Sylva said if she wasn't the Prophet, she'd try to meet you.$0#$b#Because she's the Prophet, she can't talk to someone who made the Warden a heretic.$1#$e#We told her it was Hurst's choice, but she blames you anyway. We're sorry.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.18: The spirits that follow Sylva are really funny!$0#$e#Sylva doesn't laugh at their jokes that much though. Maybe because she's Human?$8
JunimoWard.DailyPool.19: Once we tried to convince Sylva to let Hurst visit Mistgrove.$0#$e#She said no, but she seemed really sad.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.20: Sylva's eyes are the color of purple as the mists in Spring.$0#$e#That's really rare! People think it's special when that happens!$0
JunimoWard.DailyPool.21: We've lived near Mistgrove for our entire lives.$0#$e#It used to be REALLY different. Then it was weird for a bit. Then it turned into this.$0
JunimoWard.DailyPool.22: We tried to talk to the fireflies in the light jars.$0#$b#We can't speak to bugs.$1#$e#That made us sad.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.23: There aren't any paved streets in Mistgrove. It's all grass.$0#$e#It makes rolling around there a lot more comfy!$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.24: Snow surprised us when we first saw it!$2#$b#We thought the temple was the only place it happened!$3
JunimoWard.DailyPool.25: There aren't a lot of hills in Mistgrove.$0#$e#We fell down one when we were visiting the valley. We got really dizzy.$8
JunimoWard.DailyPool.26: The buildings in Mistgrove are way different from yours!$2#$e#The ones there are a mix of ruins and really big plants!$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.27: Mistgrove has a lot of spirits!$2#$e#Sometimes it's hard to even see properly!$9
JunimoWard.DailyPool.28: The Festival of Magic is one of our favorite days of the year!$2#$e#You should visit next time it happens!$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.29: Families in Mistgrove are different from yours.$0#$e#They don't get married, and they don't only have two parents. They make a big group and all work together.
JunimoWard.DailyPool.30: We sometimes visit the graveyard. We remember all of them.$0#$e#We miss them sometimes.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.31: We remember the old city in Mistgrove.$0#$e#We were still young when it was new!$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.32: Sometimes we like to explore the ruins of the old city.$0#$e#It looked really cool!.$0
JunimoWard.DailyPool.33: The ancient city was weird!$0#$e#Everyone there was like the Wizard of the Valley! Super magical! Super weird!$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.34: Do you have any friends you miss?$0#$e#We had some friends a long long time ago. The lived in the ancient city.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.35: If you ever find old ruins in the deep forests of Mistgrove, you should probably run away.$0#$e#Sometimes dangerous things live there.$3
JunimoWard.DailyPool.36: We're old. Really old.$0#$e#Our first memories stretch back to when the ancient city was just a small town.$0
JunimoWard.DailyPool.37: Whenever we get bored we sometimes wander into the ancient ruins of Mistgrove.$0#$e#We like to see how everything has changed.$0
JunimoWard.DailyPool.38: One time we got stuck in a trap left in the ruins.$0#$e#We were surprised they left behind strong enough magic to capture us!$8
JunimoWard.DailyPool.39: The Wizard is one of the only people we've met who knows about the ancient city.$0#$e#We didn't think anyone other than Hurst and Sylva knew about it!$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.40: Mistgrove is built on the outskirts of a really old city!$0#$e#The city was really magic. Then bad stuff happened.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.41: We remember the first Prophet. They were our friend.$0#$e#They asked us to protect the new townspeople.$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.42: Most people in Mistgrove think we're a story made up by their ancestors.$8#$e#Only the Prophet and Warden know we're real!$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.43: When Mistgrove first started, there was only the Prophet.$0#$e#We were the first Warden. Eventually a two Speakers were born, and the role of Warden passed to the Humans.$0
JunimoWard.DailyPool.44: Having two Speakers born at the same time is really rare!$0#$e#Usually, there's a few years between them. Hurst and Sylva are the same age though!$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.45: If only one Speaker is born, they become the Prophet.$0#$e#Until there's one to spare to be Warden, we protect the village.$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.46: The Warden is the only person in Mistgrove who deals with outsiders if they get lost in here.$0#$e#They usually just send them back outside of the woods.$0#$b#Hurst's jacket came from an outsider he became friends with!$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.47: The Prophet is really good at understanding all the spirits!$0#$e#They all talk different. Certain words mean different things depending on the spirit you talk to. It's confusing!$8
JunimoWard.DailyPool.48: The old temple is sometimes used by the Wardens as a outpost.$0#$e#There's a cellar you can only access with magic in it.$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.49: The Prophet and Warden are usually really close. Everyone else is intimidated by them!$0#$e#They usually don't form families because of that.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.50: The Warden before Hurst was really cool!$0#$e#She spoke funny, but was really good at scaring away monsters!$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.51: We don't know a lot of other junimo like us.$0#$e#A lot of them are more... mindless?$9
JunimoWard.DailyPool.52: Our friends in the community center really like gifts!$0#$e#You should bring them some!$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.53: The spirits in the Valley are a lot less talkative then the Mistgrove ones.$0#$e#It's hard to make friends with them.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.54: Do you know any other Junimo that are like us?$0#$e#We like our friends, but sometimes we wish they were... smarter.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.55: One time we tried to ask why the ocean spirits hate Hurst so much.$0#$e#They wouldn't tell us.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.56: Rarely, people from Mistgrove become spirits after they die.$0#$e#We always feel sad talking to them. Sometimes they remember themselves. Sometimes they don't.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.57: Everyone has their own different spirits.$0#$e#You can tell what they care a lot about if you can see their spirits!$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.58: Demons are scary. They're not as common in the valley, but they show up in Mistgrove often.$1#$e#The stronger ones can hurt us if we aren't careful.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.59: Most spirits are protective of Humans. They really like you guys!$2#$e#Sometimes though, there are ones that hate Humans. You should avoid those ones.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.60: There used to be other Junimo in Mistgrove. A really, really long time ago.$0#$e#...We're the only one left.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.61: We really like fish! They're so cool!$0#$e#Some of the fish in Mistgrove even glow! Isn't that cool?$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.62: Sometimes we appear to the kids in your valley!$0#$e#We like to play games with them.$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.63: The Void makes us nervous.$0#$e#Whenever we sense it, we try to run away. Scary stuff lives in there.$1
JunimoWard.DailyPool.64: We like playing pranks on people in the valley!$0#$e#Whenever we prank the people in Mistgrove, Sylva tells them it's a sign from the gods.$0
JunimoWard.DailyPool.65: The amount of people in the valley that can see us is weird!$0#$e#Are you guys all magic?$9
JunimoWard.DailyPool.66: You're a really nice person.$0#$e#Remember that.$0
JunimoWard.DailyPool.67: Have you ever seen us at your festivals?$0#$e#We usually hide behind Hurst's legs. There's a lot of people who can see us. It makes us nervous.$8
JunimoWard.DailyPool.68: We remember everyone we've ever met.$0#$e#Sometimes we check in on our old friends families. It's a way to remember them.$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.69: Outsiders aren't that common in Mistgrove.$0#$e#You need to have some magic to even get past the mists!$2
JunimoWard.DailyPool.70: We don't sleep. We do sometimes visit our Human friends while they sleep.$0#$e#Their spirits tell us what they're dreaming about.$2
WoodsmanHurst.Sig.1: I was surprised at my first sight of the Sergeant of the Lodge.$6#$e#I have never met such a tall man.$1
WoodsmanHurst.Sig.2: I made the mistake of referring to Sig by his full name.$0#$e#He did not seem very angry, but I feel guilty for the mistake. I hope he can forgive me.$2
WoodsmanHurst.Sig.3: The bees around the Sergeant always instill me with wonder.$1#$e#The way they flow about him reminds me of the spirits.$3
WoodsmanHurst.Sig.4: Do you speak with the Sergeant often? He can be quite gruff.$0#$e#I sometimes worry. Is that how I came off to others before you?$2
WoodsmanHurst.Sig.5: Piercing yourself with metal was something I did not believe was done until I saw Sig.$0#$e#I find such modification of one's body... intriguing.$1
WoodsmanHurst.Sondra.1: Sondra is quite a warm person.$0#$e#She reminds me of my Home-Mother.$1
WoodsmanHurst.Sondra.2: It surprised me to learn Sondra has been the same age for several years.$0#$e#I thought that was a gift bestowed on only the Prophets.$1
WoodsmanHurst.Sondra.3: I was quite surprised to find a cookies within my mailbox today.$0#$e#I believe Sondra left them there. I must show my thanks somehow.$1
WoodsmanHurst.Sondra.4: Ward is quite fond of Sondra as well.$0#$e#They enjoy watching the townsfolk when they visit. Sondra is one of their favorites.$3
WoodsmanHurst.Sondra.5: I found myself offered a meal by Sondra. I was leaving her some fruit as a thanks for some baked goods she left me.$0#$e#I continue to be in her debt.$1
WoodsmanHurst.Gunnar.1: I have seen Gunnar in the mines quite often.$0#$e#I believe has seen me... open a geode.$1#%Hurst mimes crushing something in between his hands.#$bAt least, the shout I heard after sounded like him.$7
WoodsmanHurst.Gunnar.2: The spirits around the potions that Gunnar makes vary wildly.$3#$e#Some of them are new even to me. I wonder if they are drawn by the potions, or created by them.$7
WoodsmanHurst.Gudrun.1: I find the respect Gudrun has for nature is quite admirable.$0#$e#It is good to see such devotion in one so young.$1
WoodsmanHurst.Nora.1: The woman Nora is quite devoted to her reptiles. She seems to have a nearly endless supply of them.$0#$e#I wonder if she would appreciate another? I encountered a strange one while gathering offerings yesterday.$7
WoodsmanHurst.NoraChameleon: I have learned much about the chameleon Nora has been searching for.$0#$b#Far more than I ever truly wished to know.$2#$b#I am glad she is happy however.$1
WoodsmanHurst.Sen.1: My first encounter with Sen was somewhat of a disaster.$0#$b#I had been unaware that those of the shadow here were peaceful.$2#e#I mistook him for a demon.$2
WoodsmanHurst.STW.1: Sometimes the cowboy looks at me like I am one of the demons themselves.$2#$e#Did I do something to offend him?$2
WoodsmanHurst.Calvin.1: The boy with the orca asked if the gods will tell me the 'winning lottery numbers'.$0#$e#I have no understanding of what he speaks of. Perhaps the spirits can help enlighten me?$8
PamCT.HurstMorrisArgument: I wonder if Morris will give us a discount to make up for that fight the other day.$1#$e#I don't care who started it! I want my discount!$3
JodiCT.HurstMorrisArgument: Hurst and Morris got into a fight in the JojaMart.$0#$b#I don't think I've seen either of them so angry before!$2
MorrisCT.HurstMorrisArgument: That miscreant Hurst is banned from all Joja premises!$3#$e#The scene he caused terrified my customers!$3
ShaneCT.HurstMorrisArgument: There was a fight at the JojaMart. I only heard bits and pieces.$0#e#I swear Hurst started speaking a different language at the end.$7
SamCT.HurstMorrisArgument: Morris totally got cussed out yesterday!$0#$e#I forgot what the fight was about though.$3
KentCT.HurstMet: You met the man in the woods?$0#$e#Something about the look in his eyes is familiar.$0
WizardCT.HurstMet: I understand you've met Mistgrove's Warden. I must say, you must be quite special.$0#$b#It's rare for him to take such interest in someone.$0
LinusCT.HurstMet: You met Hurst, didn't you?$0#$e#He's one of the few people I see in the deep forests.$0
WizardCT.HurstReligion: Ah, the Woodsman spoke of the religion of Mistgrove?$0#$e#They have a strange fascination with those connected to magic.$1
LinusCT.HurstReligion: I asked if Hurst wanted to join me in foraging recently.$0#$e#He turned me down, but seemed quite sad about it.$s
WizardCT.HurstCommandmentRenewed: The Woodsman has returned to obeying his Prophet?$0#$e#Strange. I felt the Pomegranate strengthen the more he spoke with you.$0
WizardCT.HurstStopsObeying: Hurst has told me of the events involving you and Ward. He is quite grateful to you.$0#$e#I am even futher interested in your relationship to the Junimos.$0
LinusCT.HurstStopsObeying: Hurst started speaking with me recently.$0#$e#He said he respects skills at foraging a lot, and that he's sorry for being standoffish. I wonder what's changed?$1
LeahCT.HurstStopsObeying: Hurst asked me if I'd be willing to help him with maintaining the paint on his statues. He seemed quite glad when I agreed to!$1#$b#I did worry about how he was going to pay though...$6#$e#He said he'd help me find some quality wood to carve. That's a good deal in my book!$1
RobinCT.HurstStopsObeying: Something strange happened, Hurst started conversation with me recently! He's usually so closed off.$2#$e#He asked if I can teach him ways to protect stone carvings. I gave him some wax coating to cover his shrine with.$0#$b#He said he had to repay me, so I had him chop some logs for me. We ended up talking the entire time he worked! He's more friendly than I thought.$1
KentCT.HurstStopsObeying: That man Hurst spoke with me a few days ago. He said something about spirits?$0#$b#He's actually quite friendly. He also seems to understand my...problems.$5#
WoodsmanHurst.Mail.sprinton.hurst.HurstC: Warden Hurst. ^ The gods have dispensed your Commandment. Your Commandment is to be Friendless. ^ You shall not make new friends. You may not speak to infidels more than neccessary. ^ If any Apostles are found in the godless land, you may speak to them rarely. Such is the Command of the spirits. ^ I pray you find forgiveness. ^ Prophet Sylva
WoodsmanHurst.Mail.sprinton.hurst.HurstS: I thank you for listening to my story, even if it was rather grim. ^ I apologize for causing an argument with you. I did not wish to spark anger between us. In an attempt to apologize for everything I have done to you, I have sought help from the spirits. ^ From now on, when you visit my clearings on Fridays, the spirits by my shrine should be visible. They shall have offerings for you. ^ - Hurst

Greetings @. The spirits have informed me you are truly an Apostle. ^ This means you have the option of accessing a special privilege the spirits bestow upon the Apostles. ^ If you are to visit my clearing on Fridays, the spirits beside my shrine should be visible. ^ Speak with them to learn of what this privilege entails. ^ - Hurst
WoodsmanHurst.Items.TerraGourd.Descripti: A earthy gourd with rocky skin. Rumor says it's from another dimension.
WoodsmanHurst.Items.TerraGourdSeeds.Desc: Plant these in spring. Takes 10 days to produce a Terra Gourd.
WoodsmanHurst.Items.SmolderingRose.Descr: The edges of the petals appear to be aflame. You can feel a slight heat radiating from the flower.
Plant these in the summer. Takes 11 days to produce a Smoldering Rose.
WoodsmanHurst.Items.ZephyrLily.Descripti: A vibrant yellow flower that floats in your hands.
WoodsmanHurst.Items.ZephyrLilySeeds.Desc: Plant these in the fall. Takes 11 days to produce a Zephyr Lily, and continues to produce after the first harvest.
WoodsmanHurst.Items.TeardropBerry.Descri: This juicy berry resembles a droplet of water. Legend says it comes from a land of only ice.
WoodsmanHurst.Items.TeardropBerrySeeds.D: Plant these in the winter. Takes 8 days to mature, and continues to produce after the first harvest.
WoodsmanHurst.Items.TerraSoup.Descriptio: This hearty soup makes your skin feel tough as stone.
WoodsmanHurst.Items.IfritPopRocks.Descri: After eating this, you feel as if a spirit of fire has taken over your body, strengthening you.
WoodsmanHurst.Items.SylphTea.Description: This sour tea makes you feel light as air.
WoodsmanHurst.Items.TeardropSpirits.Desc: This sweet drink makes you feel as if you hear the water speaking.
WoodsmanHurst.Items.AncientsTears.Descri: This soup fills you with a deep sorrow.
WoodsmanHurst.SpiritShopDialogue: The spirits swirl wildly around you.
ClearingStrings.HurstCrops: Even out of season, the crops are still ripe.
ClearingStrings.HurstTent: You see a small bed inside.
ClearingStrings.HurstNameGrave: The grave is marked with a list of names.
ClearingStrings.HurstSymbolGrave: The grave is marked with a symbol like a swirl of mist.
ClearingStrings.HurstCrystals: There appear to be flickering lights within the crystals.
ClearingStrings.HurstMailbox: The inside of the mailbox is coated with dust.
ClearingStrings.HurstJunimoHut: You see little bits of Starfruit deep inside the hut.
ClearingStrings.HurstBigJunimoPlush: How did Hurst get his hands on one of these?
WoodsmanHurst.SecretNotes.1: To the Prophet Sylva, from Hurst. ^ Greetings. Our mission continues deep into the woods. We have discovered many ruins, some of which I believe you would find fascinating. ^ I do wish you were able to see them. My companions remain steadfast, following me into even the darkest depths of the woods. ^ I have spoken to the spirits here. They all shirk at the mention of the demon. I fear the danger it poses. ^ I pray I will have good news for my next report. ^ -Hurst
WoodsmanHurst.SecretNotes.2: To Sylva, from Hurst. ^ I hope this report finds you well. We have come across a dilemma which I seek your guidance for. We have encountered an outsider. ^ The outsider is terrified of us, me in particular. I believe he can see the spirits and has noticed the extreme amount that one of our talents attracts. ^ He claims to be an army deserter. He begs us to not force him to return to his war. This is where I find myself conflicted. ^ I believe one of his skill could provide useful on this expedition. I will take responsibility for him if you permit me to include him in my company. ^ Yours truly, Hurst
WoodsmanHurst.SecretNotes.3: To Sylva, From Hurst. ^ The outsider has proven quite helpful in our mission. He is knowledgeable of similar excursions, and has provided knowledge for moving as a group. ^ As my only other companion in my duties was my Master, this has proven quite invaluable. ^ He has a strange coat as well. It is dark green, with a cut I have never seen before. He says it is his old military uniform. I do not know what he means by this. ^ I believe we are closing in on the demon as well. It has been nearly 5 years since our departure. I miss home. I pray we will return soon. ^ Respectfully, Hurst
WoodsmanHurst.SecretNotes.4: Dear Sylva, I pray this letter finds you well. ^ It has been quite a challenge to be this far from home for this long. I am used to such things but it remains a challenge all the same. ^ I miss you and the rest of my household. How are Sight-Mother and Spirit-Father? How about the young ones? If my estimations are correct, the Calling Ceremony is soon. ^ I hope there will not be any children as foolish as we were on our Calling day. I still keep an eye out for that demon. I have yet to see it again. ^ I keep you in my mind always. Take care of Mistgrove. I hope to return soon. ^ With love, Hurst
WoodsmanHurst.SecretNotes.5: Dearest Sylva, I miss you more with each day. ^ The deep forests are beautiful in a way I cannot put to word. You would love the ancient ruins we have discovered on our travels. ^ Maybe one day, after our Callings have been passed on we could visit here together. ^ I have grown quite fond of those sent with me. I have never been as skilled with friendships like you, but I believe you would be surprised. ^ Merri, Aera, Eon, and Runa are quite interesting people. Even the outsider Cedric is someone I have grown quite fond of. If you are not already friends with these few, I believe you would grow to love them as much as I do. ^ I still miss our conversations. I hope to be able to return soon. Take care. ^ Love, Hurst
WoodsmanHurst.SecretNotes.6: To Hurst, From Sylva. ^ Warden Hurst. I am glad you sought my guidance. Outsiders are always a challenge to deal with. ^ The spirits and I have deliberated for many days about what to do. Our initial response was to treat him like any outsider and take him to the edge of Mistgrove, but your request gave us pause. ^ If you are willing to assume responsibility for him, he may accompany you on your mission. However, if he violates any of our laws, you will both be subject to punishment. ^ Please tread carefully with him. ^ I also beg you to remain cautious when tracking the demon. Such a creature is dangerous. You know far more about their kind than I, but I still request you to remain safe. ^ Your party's safe return is more important than exorcising this foul creature. ^ - Prophet Sylva
WoodsmanHurst.SecretNotes.7: Dear Hurst. ^ I hope this letter finds you safe. I miss you greatly. ^ It has been quite some time without correspondence with you. I hope you are well. I am glad you have been able to make friends! ^ I remember our youth, when you would sit alone while I was always surrounded by friends. It is quite a surprise you and I became so close! ^ It also amuses me that our Callings seem to have been destined from our very childhoods. ^ I hope you stay safe. Please do not do anything rash. I worry you are taking your anger at the demon from our childhood out on the rumored creature. ^ Please, keep yourself and the others safe. Do not risk them, or yourself, on assuaging your guilt or settling your vendetta. ^ Love, Sylva
WoodsmanHurst.SecretNotes.8: To Sylva, From Hurst. This letter is for the eyes of the Prophet ONLY. ^ Greetings Sylva. I apologize for the rude introduction, but I have found something only you may know of. Do you recall the previous Prophet telling us of the ancient city? I have found it. ^ The demons and evil spirits seem to emerge from this place. Every single one we have encountered in the past few weeks has been unexpectedly powerful. I believe we are close to finding the creature of rumor. ^ The spirits here act strangely. They flee when I attempt to speak with them. The only words I have managed to hear from them have been warnings to turn back. ^ I will admit, I have considered it. I feel something watching us. The outsider Cedric appears to be the only other one who has felt it. I believe it is due to his past experience with such things. I plan on turning back after we reach the center of the city and repel any demons on the way there. ^ Hopefully, the straight trip back home will take only a couple months. It would be good for all of us to see home again. ^ - Hurst
WoodsmanHurst.SecretNotes.9: This letter is for the Prophet ONLY. ^ I believe we have found the source of the rumors. It is a strange distortion in the air, located in the center of the ancient city. I do not know how to deal with one this size. ^ I have encountered such things before, but they were far smaller and far easier to locate the spirits that could seal such a rift. I believe this will remain a threat, but it is not one we can do anything about with our current knowledge. ^ We will return home to seek the assistance of the spirits. ^ To be honest as well, we all miss home. Cedric has proven himself to be quite a natural fit into Mistgrove. I have undergone the proper ceremonies to induct him as one of us. He has even started to see and draw his own spirits. ^ He gave me his old military coat as thanks. In their culture, this is apparently quite a sign of respect. ^ We intend to leave on the morrow. The travel home should take us two months. We are all excited to finally return home. ^ -Hurst. ^ P.S. Cedric and I are still feeling the odd sensation of being watched. I believe it is our senses picking up on the distortion. Hopefully once we are far enough away from the distortion, the strange feelings will cease.
WoodsmanHurst.SecretNotes.10: You promised to keep yourselves safe. You PROMISED to keep yourselves safe! ^                           We're.... sorry.... we... were... too... late.... we... only... could... save... you....^                   I fear even the night. Every shadow is a beast waiting to lunge. Please spirits, take this fear away from me! ^                                                                                              Little.... squeak...... has grown..... now a...... little.... whimper?...... ha..... ha..... ha..... serves you..... right.....

TGC_FJIntroduction: The farmer meets Fr Joseph when clarence steals his sandwich
